#====================================================================================================
# START - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================

# THIS SECTION CONTAINS CRITICAL TESTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR BOTH AGENTS
# BOTH MAIN_AGENT AND TESTING_AGENT MUST PRESERVE THIS ENTIRE BLOCK

# Communication Protocol:
# If the `testing_agent` is available, main agent should delegate all testing tasks to it.
#
# You have access to a file called `test_result.md`. This file contains the complete testing state
# and history, and is the primary means of communication between main and the testing agent.
#
# Main and testing agents must follow this exact format to maintain testing data. 
# The testing data must be entered in yaml format Below is the data structure:
# 
## user_problem_statement: {problem_statement}
## backend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.py"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## frontend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.js"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## metadata:
##   created_by: "main_agent"
##   version: "1.0"
##   test_sequence: 0
##   run_ui: false
##
## test_plan:
##   current_focus:
##     - "Task name 1"
##     - "Task name 2"
##   stuck_tasks:
##     - "Task name with persistent issues"
##   test_all: false
##   test_priority: "high_first"  # or "sequential" or "stuck_first"
##
## agent_communication:
##     -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##     -message: "Communication message between agents"

# Protocol Guidelines for Main agent
#
# 1. Update Test Result File Before Testing:
#    - Main agent must always update the `test_result.md` file before calling the testing agent
#    - Add implementation details to the status_history
#    - Set `needs_retesting` to true for tasks that need testing
#    - Update the `test_plan` section to guide testing priorities
#    - Add a message to `agent_communication` explaining what you've done
#
# 2. Incorporate User Feedback:
#    - When a user provides feedback that something is or isn't working, add this information to the relevant task's status_history
#    - Update the working status based on user feedback
#    - If a user reports an issue with a task that was marked as working, increment the stuck_count
#    - Whenever user reports issue in the app, if we have testing agent and task_result.md file so find the appropriate task for that and append in status_history of that task to contain the user concern and problem as well 
#
# 3. Track Stuck Tasks:
#    - Monitor which tasks have high stuck_count values or where you are fixing same issue again and again, analyze that when you read task_result.md
#    - For persistent issues, use websearch tool to find solutions
#    - Pay special attention to tasks in the stuck_tasks list
#    - When you fix an issue with a stuck task, don't reset the stuck_count until the testing agent confirms it's working
#
# 4. Provide Context to Testing Agent:
#    - When calling the testing agent, provide clear instructions about:
#      - Which tasks need testing (reference the test_plan)
#      - Any authentication details or configuration needed
#      - Specific test scenarios to focus on
#      - Any known issues or edge cases to verify
#
# 5. Call the testing agent with specific instructions referring to test_result.md
#
# IMPORTANT: Main agent must ALWAYS update test_result.md BEFORE calling the testing agent, as it relies on this file to understand what to test next.

#====================================================================================================
# END - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================



#====================================================================================================
# Testing Data - Main Agent and testing sub agent both should log testing data below this section
#====================================================================================================

user_problem_statement: "Smart AI Product Catalog - Backend API with MongoDB and OpenAI Vision for product management and photo search"

backend:
  - task: "Health Check Endpoint"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "GET /api/ returns correct API info with version and endpoints list. Working perfectly."

  - task: "Create Category"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "POST /api/categories successfully creates categories with name and subcategories. Returns created category with ID."

  - task: "Get All Categories"
    implemented: true
    working: false
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 1
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
      - working: false
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "GET /api/categories returns 500 Internal Server Error. Root cause: MongoDB ObjectId serialization error. The endpoint returns raw MongoDB documents with _id field (ObjectId type) which is not JSON serializable. Fix needed: Convert MongoDB documents to Category models before returning, similar to get_products endpoint (lines 166-179). Error: ValueError: [TypeError('ObjectId' object is not iterable), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')]"

  - task: "Create Product"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "POST /api/products successfully creates products with all fields including base64 image. Returns created product with UUID."

  - task: "Get All Products"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "GET /api/products returns list of all products. Properly converts MongoDB documents to Product models."

  - task: "Get Product by ID"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "GET /api/products/{id} successfully retrieves single product by UUID. Returns 404 for non-existent products."

  - task: "Update Product"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "PUT /api/products/{id} successfully updates product fields. Properly handles partial updates and updates the updated_at timestamp."

  - task: "Delete Product"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "DELETE /api/products/{id} successfully deletes products. Returns proper success message and 404 for non-existent products."

  - task: "Search Products by Text"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "GET /api/products/search/text?q=query performs case-insensitive regex search on product names. Returns matching products list."

  - task: "Search Product by Barcode"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "GET /api/products/search/barcode?barcode=xxx successfully finds products by exact barcode match. Returns 404 when not found."

  - task: "AI Photo Search"
    implemented: true
    working: false
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 1
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
      - working: false
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "POST /api/products/search/photo endpoint structure is correct and returns proper response format (products array and confidence field). However, OpenAI API integration is NOT WORKING due to INVALID API KEY. Error: 401 Unauthorized - 'Incorrect API key provided: sk-emerg******************40eF'. The endpoint has error handling so it returns 200 with empty results instead of failing. The AI matching functionality is NOT operational. API key needs to be replaced with a valid OpenAI API key."

  - task: "MongoDB Integration"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: true
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "MongoDB connection and operations working correctly. Products and categories are properly stored and retrieved. Using UUID for IDs instead of ObjectId (good practice)."

frontend:
  - task: "Not tested"
    implemented: false
    working: "NA"
    file: ""
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "low"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
      - working: "NA"
        agent: "testing"
        comment: "Frontend testing not performed as per instructions. This is a React Native Expo app."

metadata:
  created_by: "testing_agent"
  version: "1.0"
  test_sequence: 1
  run_ui: false

test_plan:
  current_focus:
    - "Get All Categories"
    - "AI Photo Search"
  stuck_tasks:
    - "Get All Categories"
    - "AI Photo Search"
  test_all: false
  test_priority: "high_first"

agent_communication:
  - agent: "testing"
    message: "Backend API testing completed. 10 out of 11 endpoints working correctly. Two critical issues found: 1) Get Categories endpoint has MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug causing 500 error. 2) OpenAI API key is invalid (401 error), so AI photo matching is not functional. All CRUD operations, text search, and barcode search are working perfectly. MongoDB integration is solid."
